metadata.title: The brand_config.json: Your Brief as Machine-Readable JSON
metadata.playlist: Build the Recipe — Make Your Tool Conductor-Ready
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 5B brief — Nik's Recipe-Based Framework

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 1 of 6. Path B of Assignment 5 is called recipe-based. It is about a different kind of deliverable than a running interface. The deliverable here is a machine-readable specification that a conductor — Claude Code, a colleague, or your future self — can pick up and run without asking you any questions. Two files do most of the work. The first is the brand_config.json. It is your Conductor Brief translated into a JSON schema a machine can read. Every field in the config maps to a part of the brief. An empty field is not a placeholder — it is a signal that the brief itself is incomplete. Today you walk each field, fill it from your brief, and validate the JSON before you do anything else. Invalid JSON is ungradeable. Let's go.

props.segment: The brand_config.json: Your Brief as Machine-Readable JSON
props.command: Translate the Conductor Brief into a valid brand_config.json — field by field, then validate.
props.runningText: every field maps to a brief section — empty field = incomplete brief…
output: primary_user + data_sources + phase_gates → P2A, P3, P4+P5
Validate JSON before anything else — invalid = ungradeable

beats.narration_text: The config has six top-level fields. tool_summary is a one-to-two sentence description of what the tool does — this is Brief Part 1 in condensed form. If you cannot describe the tool in two sentences, Part 1 of the brief needs more work before you fill the config. primary_user is an object with three sub-fields: role, the job title or type of person who uses the tool; situation, a sentence describing what they are trying to do when they use it; and workaround, what they currently do before this tool existed. This maps to Brief Part 2A. data_sources is an array — one entry per source. phase_gates is an array — one entry per handoff in the pipeline. one_thing is a single sentence naming the one problem this tool solves better than any alternative — Brief Part 6B. And conductor_note is a single sentence of domain knowledge the recipe cannot capture: something a first-time conductor would not know from reading the config alone, but must know to make a judgment call.

artifactLines: tool_summary: 1–2 sentences what the tool does — from Brief P1.
primary_user: {role, situation, workaround} — from Brief P2A.
data_sources: array — one entry per Part 3 source.
phase_gates: array — one entry per pipeline handoff.
one_thing: one sentence — the one problem this solves better than any alternative (P6B).
conductor_note: one sentence of domain knowledge the recipe can't capture.
props.sparkLine: Every field maps to a brief section. Empty field = incomplete brief.

beats.narration_text: Each entry in data_sources has five fields: name, the human-readable source name; local_path, the relative path on disk where this source's verified records will live; record_type, what kind of records — articles, job postings, survey responses; update_freq, how often the source is refreshed — daily, weekly, on-demand; and quality_gate, a one-sentence rule that a record must pass to be admitted to data/verified/. That quality gate is the GIGO rule — it is the same rule you apply in Episode 2 when you build the verified folder. Write it precisely enough that the conductor can apply it without asking you what counts as passing. Each entry in phase_gates has five fields: step, the name of this handoff; good_looks_like, from Brief Part 4 — what a conductor would see if this step succeeded; bad_looks_like, from Brief Part 5 — what signals failure; response, what the conductor should do — hard stop, flag for review, log and continue; and judgment_type, one of the five codes — PA, PF, TO, IJ, or EI. A phase gate entry with no judgment_type field costs ten points per gate. The type is not optional.

artifactLines: data_sources entry: name · local_path · record_type · update_freq · quality_gate (GIGO rule).
quality_gate: precise enough to apply without asking — 'record has title + date + source field'.
phase_gates entry: step · good_looks_like (P4) · bad_looks_like (P5) · response · judgment_type.
judgment_type: PA / PF / TO / IJ / EI — one of five. Not optional.
Missing judgment_type = -10 pts per gate.
props.sparkLine: quality_gate: precise enough to apply alone. judgment_type: required, not optional.

beats.narration_text: The five judgment types are the vocabulary of accountable AI. PA — Pipeline Approval — fires when the conductor must decide whether to start or continue the run. It answers: is this safe to proceed? PF — Pipeline Failure — fires when something broke badly enough that the run must stop. It answers: the pipeline cannot continue because of this error. TO — Tool Output Review — fires when the AI produced output that must be checked by a human before it is used. It answers: does this output make sense for the user? IJ — Ingest Judgment — fires at the boundary between raw data and verified data. It answers: did the source give us what we expected in the format we expected? EI — Exception Intervention — fires on anything unexpected that does not fit the other four. It answers: something unusual happened and a human needs to decide what to do. Every phase gate in your recipe maps to one of these five. A gate that says 'human reviews here' without naming the type is telling the conductor to make a judgment call without giving them the framework to make it. That is incomplete. Ten points off.

artifactLines: PA — Pipeline Approval: is it safe to proceed? (start/continue decision)
PF — Pipeline Failure: pipeline cannot continue — hard stop.
TO — Tool Output Review: does this AI output make sense for the user?
IJ — Ingest Judgment: did the source give us what we expected in the expected format?
EI — Exception Intervention: something unexpected — human decides what to do.
'Human reviews here' with no type = incomplete = -10 pts per gate.
props.sparkLine: PA · PF · TO · IJ · EI. Name the type. Every gate.

beats.narration_text: When you finish filling the config, validate it before you do anything else. An invalid JSON file cannot be machine-parsed, which means the grader cannot use it as a machine-readable config — and the part is ungradeable. Validation takes thirty seconds. Go to a free JSON validator in your browser — jsonlint.com and json.parser.online are both widely used, and either will do. Paste the entire contents of your brand_config.json into the validator and click Validate. If you get a green check, the syntax is correct. If you get an error, the validator will tell you the line number and what the problem is — a missing comma, an unclosed bracket, a property name not in double quotes. Fix it, paste again, validate again. Common mistakes: trailing commas after the last item in an array or object — JSON does not allow them; single quotes around string values — JSON requires double quotes; unescaped backslashes inside string values. Fix each one the validator flags. A file that passes validation is not necessarily correct — the content still needs to match the brief. But a file that fails validation is definitely wrong.

props.artifactTitle: Validate — do this before anything else
artifactLines: jsonlint.com or json.parser.online — paste config, click Validate.
Green check: syntax is correct. Error: line number + what's wrong.
Common mistakes: trailing comma after last array/object item.
Single quotes → must be double quotes. Unescaped backslash in strings.
Passes validation: syntax correct. Content still needs to match the brief.
Fails validation: definitely wrong. Fix and re-validate before submitting.
props.sparkLine: Validate before anything else. Invalid = ungradeable.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for the brand_config.json. Six fields: tool_summary from Part 1, primary_user with role-situation-workaround from Part 2A, data_sources array with name-local_path-record_type-update_freq-quality_gate per source from Part 3, phase_gates array with step-good_looks_like-bad_looks_like-response-judgment_type per handoff from Parts 4 and 5, one_thing from Part 6B, and conductor_note with the one piece of domain knowledge the recipe cannot capture. Every phase gate must have a judgment_type — PA, PF, TO, IJ, or EI. A gate without a type costs ten points. Validate the JSON before doing anything else — invalid JSON is ungradeable. A vague field is a vague brief: fix the brief first, then fill the field. Episode 2 builds the verified data folder.

props.artifactTitle: The brand_config.json: Your Brief as Machine-Readable JSON
artifactLines: 6 fields: tool_summary · primary_user · data_sources · phase_gates · one_thing · conductor_note.
data_sources: name · local_path · record_type · update_freq · quality_gate per source.
phase_gates: step · good_looks_like · bad_looks_like · response · judgment_type per gate.
Judgment types: PA · PF · TO · IJ · EI. Missing = -10 pts per gate.
Validate JSON before anything else — invalid = ungradeable.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — fill in the bracketed fields from your Conductor Brief before running it. The prompt reads: I have a Conductor Brief for a Madison tool. Here is Part 1, what the tool does: [paste P1]. Here is Part 2A, the primary user: [paste role, situation, and workaround from P2A]. Here is Part 3, the data sources: [list each source name, what records it provides, and how often it updates]. Here is Part 4, what good looks like at each handoff: [paste P4]. Here is Part 5, what bad looks like at each handoff: [paste P5]. Here is Part 6B, the one thing: [paste P6B]. Generate the brand_config.json schema for this tool, filled with real values from the brief. For each phase gate, assign one judgment type from PA, PF, TO, IJ, or EI and explain in one sentence why that type applies. After generating the JSON, flag any field where the brief text was too vague to fill accurately — those fields need the brief fixed before the config is correct. Take the output, paste it into a JSON validator, fix every error, and re-validate. The config is not done until it passes validation.

props.segment: The brand_config.json: Your Brief as Machine-Readable JSON
props.command: Brief P1: [tool description]. P2A: [role + situation + workaround]. P3: [source list]. P4: [good-looks-like per handoff]. P5: [bad-looks-like per handoff]. P6B: [one thing]. Generate brand_config.json with real values. For each phase gate, assign one of PA/PF/TO/IJ/EI and explain why. Flag any field the brief text was too vague to fill.
props.runningText: paste into JSON validator after generating — fix every error, re-validate before done…

beats.narration_text: The brand_config.json: Your Brief as Machine-Readable JSON. Liam, in for Bear.
